verifications.title: Canonical lifecycle (RAW->...->REPLAYED) is normative but has no machine-enforceable stage field in the afi-config schema library
verifications.corrected_evidence: afi-config/schemas/vault.schema.json:95-98 'stageIndex' is only a boolean index hint ("description": "Create index on signal stage (RAW, ENRICHED, etc.)" at afi-config/schemas/vault.schema.json:97); the only enumerated lifecycle is in a reference repo: afi-infra/src/tssd/types.ts:8-15 'export type SignalLifecycleStage = "RAW" | "ENRICHED" | "ANALYZED" | "SCORED" | "MINTED" | "REPLAYED"'
verifications.note: Confirmed. Re-opened both citations: vault.schema.json carries no enumerated 'stage' field (only the boolean stageIndex hint), and the canonical enum lives only in the afi-infra reference repo. Original cited the enum at types.ts:10-16; re-opened it begins at types.ts:8 (RAW at :9 ... REPLAYED at :14) — corrected. Architectural-decision contradiction (lifecycle is normative law but unenforceable from the schema library), P1 stands.

verifications.title: Production gateway ingest bypasses the canonical USS/CPJ dialect (two divergent ingest contracts)
verifications.corrected_evidence: afi-gateway/src/http/app.ts:20-27 normalizeSignalPayload checks only 4 identity fields ('const required = ["signalId", "epochId", "market", "timeframe"]' at :26); afi-gateway/src/http/app.ts:134 'await vault.upsert(parsed.record)' persists without USS/CPJ validation. Contrast afi-reactor/src/server.ts:211 'const validation = validateUsignalV11(canonicalUss)' (AJV-enforced dialect)
verifications.note: Confirmed. The gateway never invokes validateUsignalV11/validateCpjV01 (those live only in afi-reactor/src/server.ts:211,314); it accepts a VaultedSignalRecord shape and upserts directly. Original cited the upsert at :135; re-opened it is at :134 — corrected by one line. P1 stands (two divergent ingest contracts drive wrong integration decisions).

verifications.title: Stated vault immutability/finality invariant is unenforced - the only canonical writer overwrites records
verifications.corrected_evidence: afi-config/docs/REGISTRIES_AND_REPUTATION.v0.1.md:111 '- Vault finality (T.S.S.D. Vault immutability) is absolute; reputation cannot rewrite history' vs afi-infra/src/tssd/MongoTSSDVaultClient.ts:150 sets 'updatedAt: nowIso' then at :157 'await this.collection.deleteOne(filter)' followed by :160 'await this.collection.insertOne(doc)' (overwrite-in-place on upsert)
verifications.note: Confirmed. Doc asserts absolute immutability; the only canonical writer's upsert (afi-infra/src/tssd/MongoTSSDVaultClient.ts:120 upsert) performs deleteOne+insertOne, i.e. destructive overwrite. Original cited 148-159; the deleteOne/insertOne pair is at :157/:160 — corrected to 150-160. P1 stands.

verifications.title: Determinism/replay invariant is unenforced at the record level: VaultedSignalRecord pins no codex/topology/version/content-hash
verifications.corrected_evidence: afi-reactor/docs/VALIDATOR_REPLAY_SPEC.v0.1.md:37 '- **Determinism:** Same codex version + same config + same data snapshot -> same validator outputs.' vs afi-infra/src/tssd/types.ts:331-367 VaultedSignalRecord (fields identity/stages/publicSurface/proprietaryDetail/training/createdAt/updatedAt only; no codex/dag-topology/scorer version or contentHash; only optional afi-infra/src/tssd/types.ts:112 'strategyVersion?: string'); afi-reactor/src/dag/DAGExecutor.ts:1003 'return `exec-${Date.now()}-${Math.random().toString(36).substring(2, 9)}`'
verifications.note: Confirmed. Re-opened VaultedSignalRecord (types.ts:331-367) carries no replay-pinning fields; only strategyVersion? at :112. Execution IDs are non-deterministic (Date.now()+Math.random() at DAGExecutor.ts:1002-1003). Determinism invariant is stated but record-level unenforced. P1 stands.

verifications.title: Canonical schema library omits the Commitment plane and the Market/analytics plane (2 of 5 protocol planes unrepresented)
verifications.corrected_evidence: afi-config/schemas/ holds vault/pipeline/blueprint/plugin/validator/USS/CPJ schemas but no on-chain receipt/anchor schema and no analytics schema; the only on-chain reference is a coordinator address/chainId field at afi-config/schemas/validatorConfig.schema.json:82-87 ('coordinatorAddress' at :82, 'chainId' at :87), not a commitment-plane schema. Reproducible negative searches (run from /home/user/AFI-Protocol): 'rg -nwi -c "bigquery|kafka|warehouse|snowflake|redshift" afi-config/schemas' => 0 hits; 'rg -nc "EmissionsMinted|mintForSignal|AFISignalReceipt|contentHash" afi-config/schemas' => 0 hits
verifications.note: Confirmed. Two scoped, collision-safe greps over afi-config/schemas return zero hits for analytics-plane and on-chain-commitment terms, so the Commitment and Market/analytics planes have no canonical schema. P1 stands (external validators cannot validate on-chain<->off-chain linkage from the schema library).

verifications.title: No content/payload hash or ruleset-version anchored on-chain - receipts cannot be cryptographically bound to off-chain evidence
verifications.corrected_evidence: afi-token/src/AFIMintCoordinator.sol:19-26 'struct MintRequest { address beneficiary; uint256 tokenAmount; uint256 receiptId; uint256 receiptAmount; bytes32 signalId; uint64 epoch; bytes extraData; }' (no hash/ruleset field); reproducible negative search: 'rg -nwi -c "contentHash|payloadHash|merkle|rulesetVersion" afi-token/src' => 0 hits
verifications.note: Confirmed. Re-opened the MintRequest struct (the only on-chain mint payload) — no contentHash/payloadHash/rulesetVersion; signalId/epoch/extraData only. Scoped grep over afi-token/src returns 0 hash/ruleset hits. Original cited struct 19-27; it closes at :26 — corrected to 19-26. P1 stands.

verifications.title: Mint legitimacy gated only by EMISSIONS_ROLE; on-chain enforces no scoring/finality, so an external validator must fully trust the role holder
verifications.corrected_evidence: afi-token/src/AFIToken.sol:92 'function mintEmissions(address beneficiary, uint256 amount) external onlyRole(EMISSIONS_ROLE)' (only on-chain gate besides the :97 'if (totalSupply() + amount > TOTAL_SUPPLY_CAP)' cap); afi-token/script/DeployAFITokenMainnet.s.sol:62-63 'address admin_ = treasurySafe; address emissionsController_ = treasurySafe;'
verifications.note: Confirmed. mintEmissions enforces only EMISSIONS_ROLE + the 86B cap; no scoring/finality check exists on-chain. Pattern A grants both admin and emissions to a single Treasury Safe. Considered upgrading to P0 (verifiability gap) but a conforming external validator can still interoperate; it merely cannot independently attest scoring legitimacy, which is a doc/code trust contradiction. P1 stands.

verifications.title: Canonical VaultedSignalRecord carries no pinned version / content-hash fields, so replays cannot be made deterministic or challenge-ready
verifications.corrected_evidence: afi-infra/src/tssd/types.ts:331-367 'export interface VaultedSignalRecord' (identity/stages/publicSurface/proprietaryDetail/training/createdAt/updatedAt; no DAG-topology/plugin/scorer/codex version or contentHash); only afi-infra/src/tssd/types.ts:112 'strategyVersion?: string'
verifications.note: Confirmed (shares root cause with theme:A-normative-surface#3 and draft:53). Re-opened the full interface 331-367: no replay-pinning fields. P1 stands.

verifications.title: Reactor's scoring output bypasses the canonical vault: tssdVaultService writes a divergent ReactorScoredSignalDocument to a separate collection, never stages.scored
verifications.corrected_evidence: afi-reactor/src/services/tssdVaultService.ts:6 'ISOLATION: This service uses Reactor-owned collections, isolated from afi-infra TSSD vault.'; afi-reactor/src/services/tssdVaultService.ts:59 'const collectionName = process.env.AFI_MONGO_COLLECTION_SCORED || "reactor_scored_signals_v1"'; afi-reactor/src/types/ReactorScoredSignalV1.ts:67 'export interface ReactorScoredSignalDocument' (analystScore at :98, rawUss at :117) is a different shape than afi-infra/src/tssd/types.ts:331 VaultedSignalRecord
verifications.note: Confirmed. The reactor explicitly isolates its scored output into reactor_scored_signals_v1 with a divergent document type; the canonical VaultedSignalRecord.stages.scored is never populated by reactor. Two parallel vaults. P1 stands.

verifications.title: Public/proprietary separation is structural only - proprietaryDetail is persisted plaintext in the same document and returned by every read
verifications.corrected_evidence: afi-infra/src/tssd/MongoTSSDVaultClient.ts:278-281 toDb spreads the whole record into the BSON doc ('...record' at :280, incl. proprietaryDetail); afi-infra/src/tssd/MongoTSSDVaultClient.ts:163-191 getBySignalId/query return the full document with no projection; afi-infra/src/tssd/TSSDVaultClient.ts:88 'No persistence, encryption, or access control is handled by this class.'
verifications.note: Confirmed. toDb (:278) spreads the entire record (proprietaryDetail included) and reads (getBySignalId :163, query :171) apply no projection. Separation is structural only. P1 stands.

verifications.title: Two divergent DAG definitions: the 'canonical' codex DAG lists nodes the runtime Froggy pipeline explicitly marks REMOVED
verifications.corrected_evidence: afi-reactor/config/dag.codex.json:1-127 (8-node Scarlet chain; legacy alpha-scout/structurer/execution-sim purged 2026-06 per PR #29) vs afi-reactor/src/config/froggyPipeline.ts:96-151 (6-stage FROGGY_TREND_PULLBACK_PIPELINE); README.md:122 still calls dag.codex.json 'canonical orchestrator config'
verifications.note: Partially resolved by 2026-06 legacy purge: dead nodes no longer appear in dag.codex.json. Remaining drift is two unrelated graphs (8-node codex vs 6-stage runtime Froggy). Downgraded P1→P2; citation ranges updated post-purge (file now 127 lines).

verifications.title: afi-reactor self-declares as THE 'ONLY' orchestrator and 'the DAG is law', presenting the reference scoring-DAG plane as protocol law
verifications.corrected_evidence: afi-reactor/README.md:137 '**afi-reactor is the ONLY orchestrator in AFI Protocol.**'; afi-reactor/AGENTS.md:3 'This is the **ONLY orchestrator** in the AFI ecosystem'; afi-reactor/docs/AFI_ORCHESTRATOR_DOCTRINE.md:38-40 '### 3. The DAG is law ... ad-hoc flows are anti-patterns' vs afi-docs/specs/AFI_PORTABLE_PROTOCOL_SURFACE.v0.1.md:53 'afi-reactor, custom DAG, Mage blocks (if outputs conform)'
verifications.note: Confirmed verbatim at all three reactor sites; North Star (:53, :68 'any conforming orchestrator') frames the scoring-DAG plane as pluggable. Reference-as-law contradiction that misdirects architecture. P1 stands (shared with draft:83).

verifications.title: Canonical emissions schedule is copy-pasted (inlined) into afi-mint instead of imported, creating drift risk in the replay-critical mint path
verifications.corrected_evidence: afi-mint/src/adapters/EmissionsMintDataProvider.ts:19 '// Inlined from afi-math to avoid circular dependency issues.' with duplicate constant afi-mint/src/adapters/EmissionsMintDataProvider.ts:51 'cap: 86_000_000_000n' vs canonical afi-math/src/emissions/emissionsSchedule.ts:60 'cap: 86_000_000_000n'
verifications.note: Confirmed. The afi-mint provider inlines (copy-pastes) the afi-math schedule with duplicated constants rather than importing it; emissions ruleset version is not pinned in the receipt, so drift in the replay-critical path is silent. P1 stands.

verifications.title: Documented goldpaper mint formula is not the implemented formula (B(t) clamp model replaced by proportional epoch-budget allocation)
verifications.corrected_evidence: afi-mint/src/adapters/EmissionsMintDataProvider.ts:11 'ΔAFIᵢ = clamp(B(t) × Qᵢ × Nᵢ × R_val,i × E_epoch, 0.5×B(t), 2.0×B(t))' (docstring) vs afi-mint/src/adapters/EmissionsMintDataProvider.ts:277-281 'const proportionalShare = signalWeight / totalWeight; const baseAmount = remainingBudget * proportionalShare; ... const adjustedAmount = baseAmount * this.config.epochPulseFactor;' (the documented B(t) constant is set at :202 'baseMultiplier: 8.0' but unused in the amount path)
verifications.note: Confirmed. The implemented amount path is proportional epoch-budget allocation, not the documented clamp(B(t)*...) model; baseMultiplier B(t)=8 (:202) never enters the amount calculation. Doc/code formula contradiction. P1 stands.

verifications.title: Snapshot governance vote and reputation/epoch-pulse multipliers can override the deterministic validator decision and mint amount
verifications.corrected_evidence: afi-mint/src/orchestrator/SignalStateManager.ts:284-286 'if (challengeSucceeded) { ... finalDecision = state.validatorDecision === \'qualified\' ? \'reject\' : \'mint\'; }'; afi-mint/src/adapters/EmissionsMintDataProvider.ts:272-273 'const R = metadata.reputationWeight; const signalWeight = Q * N * R;' and :281 'const adjustedAmount = baseAmount * this.config.epochPulseFactor;'
verifications.note: Confirmed. A successful challenge flips the finalDecision (mint<->reject) and the mint amount is scaled by reputationWeight R and the governance epochPulseFactor. Governance/reputation can override the deterministic outcome and amount. P1 stands (shared with draft:66 and theme:H-governance#1).

verifications.title: On-chain emissions legitimacy is gated only by EMISSIONS_ROLE (held by one Treasury Safe); no schedule/epoch-cap/score is enforced on-chain
verifications.corrected_evidence: afi-token/src/AFIToken.sol:92 'function mintEmissions(address beneficiary, uint256 amount) external onlyRole(EMISSIONS_ROLE)'; afi-token/src/AFIToken.sol:97 'if (totalSupply() + amount > TOTAL_SUPPLY_CAP)'; afi-token/script/DeployAFITokenMainnet.s.sol:62-63 'address admin_ = treasurySafe; address emissionsController_ = treasurySafe;'
verifications.note: Confirmed. The only on-chain emissions invariants are EMISSIONS_ROLE and the 86B TOTAL_SUPPLY_CAP; no per-epoch schedule/cap or score is enforced. Treasury Safe holds the role. P1 stands.

verifications.title: On-chain mint is single-beneficiary; the multi-role gauge/splits model exists only in research (afi-econ) with no contract or production path
verifications.corrected_evidence: afi-token/src/AFIMintCoordinator.sol:19-26 'struct MintRequest { address beneficiary; ... }' and :76 'token.mintEmissions(req.beneficiary, req.tokenAmount);' (single payee) vs afi-econ/params/gauge_v0.yaml:5-8 'producers: 0.55 / enrichment: 0.25 / validators: 0.10 / public_goods: 0.10'; afi-econ/README.md:24-26 '- Production parameter decisions ... - Whitepaper claims ... - Smart contract configuration' under '**Do NOT use for**:'
verifications.note: Confirmed. On-chain mint targets one beneficiary per signal; the role-based split/gauge model exists only in the afi-econ research kit, which itself disclaims production/contract use. Original cited gauge 4-11; the split values are at :5-8 — corrected. P1 stands (shared with theme:C and draft register mint-model tension).

verifications.title: Emissions mint amount is scaled by an unpinned reputationWeight and governance-controlled epochPulseFactor, making allocation non-replayable
verifications.corrected_evidence: afi-mint/src/adapters/EmissionsMintDataProvider.ts:272-273 'const R = metadata.reputationWeight; const signalWeight = Q * N * R;' and :281 'const adjustedAmount = baseAmount * this.config.epochPulseFactor;'; R source unwired ('rg -nc "implements ISignalMetadataFetcher" afi-mint/src' => 0 hits; interface declared at afi-mint/src/adapters/EmissionsMintDataProvider.ts:164). Neither value is anchored in the on-chain receipt: AFISignalReceipt event afi-token/src/AFISignalReceipt.sol:15 'event ReceiptMinted(address indexed to, uint256 indexed id, uint256 amount, bytes data)' and afi-token/src/AFIMintCoordinator.sol:19-26 MintRequest carry no reputation/epochPulse field
verifications.note: Confirmed with field-level cites (citation-precision nit #4). The mint amount is scaled by reputationWeight R and epochPulseFactor; the R provider has no implementation (collision-safe scoped grep 'implements ISignalMetadataFetcher' over afi-mint/src returns 0 hits, default 1.0). Re-opened both on-chain receipt surfaces (AFISignalReceipt.sol:15 ReceiptMinted, AFIMintCoordinator.sol:19-26 MintRequest) — neither pins reputation or epochPulse, so the allocation is non-replayable. P1 stands.

verifications.title: Public gateway ingest performs only a 4-field presence check, not canonical USS/CPJ/vault-schema validation, before writing to the vault
verifications.corrected_evidence: afi-gateway/src/http/app.ts:26-27 'const required = ["signalId", "epochId", "market", "timeframe"]; const missing = required.filter((k) => !identity[k]);' then afi-gateway/src/http/app.ts:44 'stages: payload.stages ?? {}' and :51 'proprietaryDetail: payload.proprietaryDetail' pass through unvalidated, and afi-gateway/src/http/app.ts:134 'await vault.upsert(parsed.record)'
verifications.note: Confirmed. The public ingest validates only 4 identity fields, forwards stages/publicSurface/proprietaryDetail unvalidated, then upserts. No USS/CPJ/vault-schema validation at the boundary. P1 stands (shared with theme:A#1 and draft:43).

verifications.title: Two disjoint public ingest surfaces with inconsistent validation; no single canonical public protocol API
verifications.corrected_evidence: afi-gateway/src/http/app.ts:123 'app.post("/api/v1/signals", auth, ...)' (presence-check only, Mongo write at :134) vs afi-reactor/src/server.ts:159 'app.post("/api/webhooks/tradingview", ...)' and afi-reactor/src/server.ts:290 'app.post("/api/ingest/cpj", ...)' (AJV USS/CPJ validation at :211,:314,:356)
verifications.note: Confirmed. Two public ingress points with incompatible notions of a valid signal: gateway 4-field presence check vs reactor AJV USS/CPJ enforcement. No single canonical public API. P1 stands.

verifications.title: 'Validator certification moved to external certification layer' but no external-validator service, endpoint, or interface exists
verifications.corrected_evidence: afi-gateway/src/afiClient.ts:46 '- Validator certification (moved to external certification layer)'; afi-reactor/src/config/froggyPipeline.ts:93 '- validator-decision: Moved to external certification layer (not Reactor's responsibility)'; afi-reactor/src/types/ReactorScoredSignalV1.ts:8 'Validator certification, execution, and minting are NOT Reactor's responsibility.' Reproducible negative search (from /home/user/AFI-Protocol): 'rg -ni "external certification layer|certification service|certificationEndpoint" afi-gateway/src afi-reactor/src afi-config/schemas' => only 4 doc-comment hits (afi-gateway/src/afiClient.ts:46, afi-reactor/src/server.ts:15, afi-reactor/src/config/froggyPipeline.ts:93, afi-reactor/src/services/froggyDemoService.ts:16), zero service/endpoint definitions
verifications.note: Confirmed (citation-precision nit #3). Three sites declare certification 'moved to an external layer', but a scoped collision-safe grep over afi-gateway/src, afi-reactor/src and afi-config/schemas finds only comments referencing the moved-away layer and the INTERNAL validatorConfig.schema.json — no external-validator service, endpoint, or interop schema. The portable spec's promised third-party Replay Contract is undelivered. P1 stands (borderline P0 for third-party interop, but kept at P1 as a missing-feature/contradiction gap).

verifications.title: Official SDKs (afi-sdk-python, afi-sdk-ts) are empty 1.0.0-versioned scaffolds with non-functional usage examples
verifications.corrected_evidence: afi-sdk-python/pyproject.toml:3 'version = "1.0.0"' with only pyproject.toml + README.md present (no afi_sdk package dir); afi-sdk-python/README.md:28 'from afi_sdk import AFIClient' (import cannot resolve); afi-sdk-ts/package.json:6 '"main": "./dist/index.js"' (no dist; only package.json + README.md) and afi-sdk-ts/package.json:16 '"test": "echo 'Test script TBD'"'
verifications.note: Confirmed. Directory listings show each SDK contains only a manifest + README (no source/dist), yet both are versioned 1.0.0 and the READMEs show install/import that immediately fail. P1 stands (external builders following the docs hit import/install failures).

verifications.title: Vault schema lacks canonical lifecycle stage enum despite stages being indexed
verifications.corrected_evidence: afi-config/schemas/vault.schema.json:95-98 'stageIndex' is a boolean (afi-config/schemas/vault.schema.json:97 "description": "Create index on signal stage (RAW, ENRICHED, etc.)"); no enumerated stage field exists in the schema, while the enum lives only in afi-infra/src/tssd/types.ts:8-15
verifications.note: Confirmed. Same finding as theme:A-normative-surface#0 verified independently: the canonical schema indexes 'stage' but never enumerates the RAW->REPLAYED values. P1 stands.

verifications.title: No analytics/market plane and no on-chain commitment schema in the canonical schema library
verifications.corrected_evidence: afi-config/schemas/ — the only on-chain reference is a coordinator address/chainId at afi-config/schemas/validatorConfig.schema.json:82-87 ('coordinatorAddress' at :82, 'chainId' at :87), not a commitment-plane schema, and there is no analytics schema. Reproducible negative searches (from /home/user/AFI-Protocol): 'rg -nwi -c "bigquery|kafka|warehouse|snowflake|redshift" afi-config/schemas' => 0 hits; 'rg -nc "EmissionsMinted|mintForSignal|AFISignalReceipt|contentHash" afi-config/schemas' => 0 hits
verifications.note: Confirmed (matches theme:A-normative-surface#4). Scoped collision-safe greps over afi-config/schemas show neither an analytics-plane schema nor an on-chain commitment/anchor schema exists. P1 stands.

verifications.title: CI never runs schema validation (script name mismatch)
verifications.corrected_evidence: afi-config/.github/workflows/ci.yml:21-22 '- name: Validate configs\n        run: npm run validate:config --if-present || echo "No validation script"' but afi-config/package.json:13 defines '"validate": "vitest run tests/schema-validation.test.ts"' (no 'validate:config' script); --if-present silently skips the missing script
verifications.note: Confirmed. The CI step invokes 'npm run validate:config --if-present', but package.json defines 'validate' (not 'validate:config'); with --if-present the step is a silent no-op, so schema validation never runs in CI despite the step name. P1 stands (false CI assurance can let schema-breaking changes merge).

verifications.title: Stated client-only / no-direct-DB principle contradicted by direct Mongo vault write
verifications.corrected_evidence: afi-gateway/AGENTS.md:89 '- **No direct DB access**: All AFI data access happens via AFI HTTP/WS APIs.' vs afi-gateway/src/http/app.ts:133-134 'const vault = vaultFactory(tenantId); await vault.upsert(parsed.record);' (vaultFactory yields a Mongo-backed TSSD client that writes directly)
verifications.note: Confirmed (citation-precision nit #2: exact AGENTS.md anchor). The gateway's own AGENTS.md (also :133, :178) states a client-only / no-direct-DB principle, yet the production ingest path constructs a vault client and upserts straight to Mongo. P1 stands.

verifications.title: Ingest boundary does not validate against canonical USS/CPJ dialect
verifications.corrected_evidence: afi-gateway/src/http/app.ts:26-27 'const required = ["signalId", "epochId", "market", "timeframe"]; const missing = required.filter((k) => !identity[k]);' is the only inbound check before afi-gateway/src/http/app.ts:134 'await vault.upsert(parsed.record)'; no validateUsignalV11/validateCpjV01 call exists in afi-gateway/src/http/app.ts
verifications.note: Confirmed (matches theme:I-sdks-gateway#0 / theme:A#1). The gateway boundary performs only a 4-field presence check, never USS/CPJ schema validation, before persisting. P1 stands.

verifications.title: TSSD vault production path is Mongo-only; multi-engine vault is absent
verifications.corrected_evidence: afi-infra/src/tssd/TSSDVaultClient.ts:200 '"[TSSD] AFI_TSSD_MONGODB_URI is required in production. Falling back to in-memory is not allowed."' (prod hard-fail to Mongo) vs afi-config/schemas/vault.schema.json:13-22 engine enum '"mongodb", "postgresql", "timescaledb", "influxdb"' — only the mongodb client (afi-infra/src/tssd/MongoTSSDVaultClient.ts) implements ITSSDVaultClient
verifications.note: Confirmed (citation-precision nit #1: direct engine-enum anchor at vault.schema.json:13-22). Production requires Mongo and forbids any fallback; the schema advertises four engines but only mongodb has an implementation. Original cited TSSDVaultClient.ts:199; the prod hard-fail string is at :200 — corrected. P1 stands.

verifications.title: Canonical vault type omits pinned-version / determinism replay fields
verifications.corrected_evidence: afi-infra/src/tssd/types.ts:331-367 'export interface VaultedSignalRecord' has identity/stages/publicSurface/proprietaryDetail/training/createdAt/updatedAt and no codex/dag-topology/scorer version or contentHash field; only optional afi-infra/src/tssd/types.ts:112 'strategyVersion?: string'
verifications.note: Confirmed (same as theme:D-evidence-vault#0 / theme:A#3, verified independently). Re-opened interface 331-367 carries no replay-pinning fields. P1 stands.

verifications.title: Two divergent SignalValidatorState state machines (Zod schema vs TS type)
verifications.corrected_evidence: afi-mint/schemas/SignalValidatorState.schema.ts:13-20 'z.enum(["pending","decay_pass","challenge_open","voting_complete","minted","rejected"])' vs the TS state machine afi-mint/src/orchestrator/SignalStateManager.ts:42-52 VALID_TRANSITIONS keyed on SignalValidatorStateKind = pending/qualified/rejected/challenge_window/contested/dispute_resolved/finalized/minted/rejected_final
verifications.note: Confirmed. The Zod schema and the TS transition map enumerate disjoint state sets: Zod-only = decay_pass, challenge_open, voting_complete; TS-only = qualified, challenge_window, contested, dispute_resolved, finalized, rejected_final. Two divergent state machines for the same concept. P1 stands.

verifications.title: Reputation/governance can override deterministic mint outcome and amount
verifications.corrected_evidence: afi-mint/src/orchestrator/SignalStateManager.ts:284-286 'if (challengeSucceeded) { ... finalDecision = state.validatorDecision === \'qualified\' ? \'reject\' : \'mint\'; }' and afi-mint/src/adapters/EmissionsMintDataProvider.ts:272-273,281 (reputationWeight R into signalWeight; baseAmount * epochPulseFactor)
verifications.note: Confirmed (same mechanism as theme:G-emissions-mint#2 / theme:H-governance#1). A successful Snapshot challenge flips the mint/reject outcome and reputation/epochPulse scale the amount. P1 stands.

verifications.title: Reactor self-declares as THE single orchestrator, contradicting portable-protocol pluggability
verifications.corrected_evidence: afi-reactor/README.md:137 '**afi-reactor is the ONLY orchestrator in AFI Protocol.**'; afi-reactor/docs/AFI_ORCHESTRATOR_DOCTRINE.md:38-40 '### 3. The DAG is law ... ad-hoc flows are anti-patterns' vs afi-docs/specs/AFI_PORTABLE_PROTOCOL_SURFACE.v0.1.md:53 'afi-reactor, custom DAG, Mage blocks (if outputs conform)' and :68 'any conforming orchestrator'
verifications.note: Confirmed (same as theme:E-scoring-dag#1, verified independently). Reactor's README/Doctrine assert single-orchestrator/DAG-is-law; the portable spec makes the scoring-DAG plane pluggable. P1 stands.

verifications.title: Vault layer hard-bound to MongoDB; no multi-engine seam at the persistence layer
verifications.corrected_evidence: afi-reactor/src/services/tssdVaultService.ts:4-17 '* Provides MongoDB persistence for scored signals ... * - AFI_MONGO_URI: MongoDB connection string (required)' (Mongo client created at :57 'const uri = process.env.AFI_MONGO_URI'); afi-infra production hard-fail at afi-infra/src/tssd/TSSDVaultClient.ts:200 vs the four-engine enum afi-config/schemas/vault.schema.json:13-22
verifications.note: Confirmed. The reactor vault service is MongoDB-only (AFI_MONGO_URI required) with no engine abstraction; corroborates the infra Mongo-only hard-fail (TSSDVaultClient.ts:200) against the schema's four-engine enum. P1 stands (shared Mongo-only tension with draft:52 and theme:B/D).
